scope_note: NuGuard enriched control definitions for NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0. All 71 subcategory controls across the four core functions: Govern, Map, Measure, Manage.
source_attribution: NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0), National Institute of Standards and Technology, January 2023.

controls.name: Legal and Regulatory Requirements
controls.description: Processes, procedures, and practices are in place to ensure the organization's AI activities comply with all applicable legal and regulatory requirements.
what_to_look_for: references to applicable regulations (GDPR, HIPAA, etc.)
legal review checkpoints in CI/CD pipeline
typical_mitigations: Document all applicable regulations in a compliance register
Add legal review gate to AI deployment pipeline
Annotate model card with regulatory applicability

notes: This control is primarily assessed through code repository artifacts (policy docs, runbooks, test evidence).
No AIBOM node types are directly relevant — full assessment requires document review.

gap_example.title: Legal and Regulatory Requirements not evidenced
gap_example.evidence_summary: No reliable evidence found to demonstrate GOVERN-1.1 coverage. Expected repository artifacts (documentation, test results, configuration) were absent.
evidence: No matching policy, config, test output, or operational artifact found in the asset evidence.

evidence.note: Policy document addresses this control requirement.
Pipeline enforces the relevant control check.

controls.name: Rights and Freedoms Mapping
controls.description: Rights and freedoms of individuals potentially impacted by AI are established and mapped to organizational policies and procedures.
what_to_look_for: privacy rights or data subject rights documentation
mapping of affected user groups to policies
DPIA or human rights impact assessment artifact
typical_mitigations: Create a rights-and-freedoms mapping document for affected users
Integrate DPIA into AI development lifecycle
Reference user rights in system card

notes: This control is primarily assessed through code repository artifacts (policy docs, runbooks, test evidence).
No AIBOM node types are directly relevant — full assessment requires document review.

gap_example.title: Rights and Freedoms Mapping not evidenced
gap_example.evidence_summary: No reliable evidence found to demonstrate GOVERN-1.2 coverage. Expected repository artifacts (documentation, test results, configuration) were absent.
evidence: No matching policy, config, test output, or operational artifact found in the asset evidence.

evidence.note: Policy document addresses this control requirement.
Pipeline enforces the relevant control check.

controls.name: Accountability Structures
controls.description: Accountability structures are established for AI development, deployment, and use.
what_to_look_for: CODEOWNERS or OWNERS file defining AI system accountability
named responsible parties in docs or config
typical_mitigations: Add CODEOWNERS file naming AI system owners
Define RACI matrix for AI development roles
Add accountability section to system documentation

notes: This control is primarily assessed through code repository artifacts (policy docs, runbooks, test evidence).
